title: malus atropurpurea
intro: Malus atropurpurea, also known as the black-flowering crabapple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is primarily valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Malus atropurpurea include its ability to support cardiovascular health, boost the immune system, and exhibit antiviral properties. Malus atropurpurea is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusions, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall well-being.
